Includes 11 pp. from book about Wookies with "Star Wars" author Brian Daley's name on Xerox; interview between George Lucas and "Carol" about Han Solo in August 1977; interview between "C-3PO" (likely Lucas) and "Carol" in July 1977; and interview between "Princess Leia" (identified as Lucas) and "Carol" in August 1977.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pp. Xeroxed printed typescript outlining art panels for "Star Wars" comics, June 1992.

did.unittitle: "Darksider: The Myth of Evil in Star Wars and other films of G. Lucas"
Dark Empire / Dark Empire II / Empire's End

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for these projects in the notebooks within Series IV.

did.unittitle: Early concept work for Dark Empire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript, Xeroxed typescript, and comic book panels, with autograph and holograph annotations throughout. Includes early drafts of material that would become the "Dark Empire" series, with plot summary of "Dark Power of the Empire" and glossary of terms. Xeroxed materials include extensive typewritten letter from Veitch to Cam Kennedy outlining responses to proposed artwork, collaged Xerox cutouts from "Comics Buyers Guide," no. 808 with a feature on Veitch/Kennedy collaboration, and 23 pp. excerpt from unspecified "Star Wars" comic.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Electric typescript drafts of story and dialogue for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," many with autograph and holograph annotations; Xeroxed and printed copies of page art by Cam Kennedy; faxed drafts of work; side-stapled holograph page layouts; and autograph and holograph drafts of the work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 pp. autograph notes on "Star Wars: Dark Empire" proposal; 11 pp. printed typescript, "Notes on proposed STAR WARS story by Veitch/Kennedy"; 16 pp. printed typescript, "Synopses of the Epic limited series"; 19 pp. Xerox copy of printed typescript, "Synopses of the Epic limited series"; 3 pp. autograph panel layout; 20 pp. printed typescript, "Star Wars - by Veitch/Kennedy"; Xerox of announcement for Veitch/Kennedy collaboration; autograph notes and panel layout; 17pp. printed typescript, "Star Wars: Dark Empire," story summary; 16 pp. printed typescript, "Star Wars: Dark Empire"; 15 pp. Xeroxed printed typescript, "Star Wars: Dark Empire"; 15 pp. Xeroxed printed typescript, "Star Wars: Dark Empire"; Xeroxed holographic note on "Hilroy Stormbringer," author unknown. Autograph and holograph annotations present throughout on almost all documents.

did.unittitle: "STAR WARS - Book #2," Dark Empire #2 early scripts and panel layout

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescripts with autograph annotations throughout. Includes early "Dark Empire" drafts from 1990 with notes from Veitch to "Check for unused ideas." Includes stapled booklet of autograph panel mockup layouts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 15 pp. printed typescript draft of script for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 2.

did.unittitle: "STAR WARS - #1," Dark Empire #1 script and editor comments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 pp. printed typescript draft of script for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 1. With postcard from Barbara Kesel discussing draft.

did.unittitle: "STAR WARS - ISSUE #2," Dark Empire #2 scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed and Xeroxed drafts of work related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 2 with light autograph and holograph annotations throughout. Xeroxed drafts include artwork with script.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed and Xeroxed typescript from "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 3 with autograph annotations throughout. Autograph opening crawl text on legal-size paper included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph draft of opening crawl text for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 4. Includes also printed typescript and Xeroxed typescript drafts of work related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 4, with letterer's scripts, many with significant autograph annotation throughout. With some Xeroxed fax correspondence with Barbara Kesel and Dark Horse Comics, and Xeroxed advertisements for "Star Wars: Dark Empire" comics with sticky note: "For CAM."

did.unittitle: "STAR WARS: DARK EMPIRE," issue #1 page mockups

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black plastic three-ring binder, assembled by Veitch, containing approx. 145 pp. full-color printed page mockups for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 1 by Veitch and Cam Kennedy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and Xeroxed page mock-ups with art and script for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 5. Includes handwritten revisions throughout. There are 5 separate drafts, of which 4 are corner-stapled and one is unbound.

did.unittitle: "DARK EMPIRE - ISSUE #5," scripts and editor comments

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 25 pp. autograph panel mockup layout for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 5, on dot matrix paper folded once to create a booklet; printed typescript with significant autograph annotations throughout. Some outgoing Xeroxed fax correspondence with Dark Horse Comics included, along with shipping slips for materials sent to Barbara Kesel at Dark Horse Comics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 25 pp. autograph panel mockup layout for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 6 on dot matrix paper, folded once to create a booklet; approx. 46 pp. printed typescript drafts of work for issues no. 5 and 6 of "Star Wars: Dark Empire," along with 19 pp. Xeroxed drafts of work from issues no. 5 and 6; autograph page of panel layouts along with Xeroxed typewritten letter, signed, from Veitch to Barbara Kesel. Includes shipping slips for materials sent to Cam Kennedy and Barbara Kesel.

did.unittitle: "STAR WARS DARK EMPIRE TEXT PAGES + TECH READOUT"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black plastic three-ring binder, assembled by Veitch, with printed typescript drafts of work from "Star Wars: Dark Empire." Printed storyboards and tech readouts, with printed typescript glossary and approx. 8 pp. Xeroxed press ephemera.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black plastic three-ring binder, assembled by Veitch, containing printed and Xeroxed typescript glossary pages produced by Veitch and collaborator Cam Kennedy for their "Star Wars: Dark Empire" stories. Glossaries are divided into sections within the binder with autograph tabs. Glossaries of images contain collaged content by Veitch. There are also some loose leaves, including approx. 8 pp. Xeroxed "Star Wars: Dark Empire no. 5 pencils" drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript, along with corner-stapled letterer's script for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," no. 6, dated August 21 1992. Draft of opening crawl text, with "Final Rollup" written in marker across the top.

did.unittitle: "SWDE II #1 color copies of ART w/o dialog"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manila letter-size envelope with clasps housing approx. 24 pp. full-color printed illustrated storyboards for "Star Wars: Dark Empire," without script.

did.unittitle: "SW: DE II Issue #6," scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II," no. 6. Includes 25 pp. side-stapled autograph panel mockup; 4 pp. panel layout along with several pages of autograph notes; printed typescript drafts of proposals for plot with autograph and holographs annotations throughout; correspondence providing feedback from Dark Horse and Lucasfilm; and Xeroxes of proposed artwork and panel layouts, with and without lettering, with include numerous handwritten revisions.

did.unittitle: "SWDE," Empire's End #1 lettered art and Dark Empire reference material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript with some full-color printed artwork and Xeroxed typescript, with drafts of material from "Star Wars: Dark Empire," nos. 4–6, including opening crawl text, plot summaries, and artwork.

did.unittitle: "SWDE," Empire's End #1 lettered art and Dark Empire reference material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript with some full-color printed artwork and Xeroxed typescript, with drafts of material from "Star Wars: Dark Empire," nos. 4–6, including opening crawl text, plot summaries, and artwork.

did.unittitle: Dark Empire Sourcebook manuscript by West End Games

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manila envelope housing printed typescript draft of "Dark Empire Sourcebook" by Bill Smith. Veitch has annotated in red pen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder containing: full notebook, 40 leaves, titled "Graphic Story Album Part Two," with autograph drafts; full notebook, 40 leaves, titled "GSA 3," with autograph drafts; shipping slip for documents sent to Cam Kennedy; printed drafts of work related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II," with autograph annotation and storyboards with pencil notations, accompanied by Xeroxed page art.

did.unittitle: "SW: DE II Issue #2," scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II," no. 2. Includes 6 pp. autograph panel layouts; 5 pp. autograph chapter outlines; printed typescripts of opening crawl text; drafts of proposed changes sent to Cam Kennedy by Veitch; Xeroxed artwork by Kennedy, much with autograph annotations; full-color prints; and shipping slip from Veitch to Kennedy.

did.unittitle: "SW: DE II Issue #3," scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II" no. 3. Includes 6 pp. autograph panel layouts; 5 pp. autograph chapter outlines; printed typescript drafts of opening crawl text and story proposals; typewritten letter from Veitch to Kennedy regarding their working partnership and relationship with Dark Horse, on top of which Veitch has written "Not Sent Per T.J."; Xeroxed art and scripts with autograph annotations throughout; 2 pp. change request from Dark Horse Comics; and shipping slips.

did.unittitle: "SW: DE II Issue #3," scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II" no. 3. Includes 6 pp. autograph panel layouts; 5 pp. autograph chapter outlines; printed typescript drafts of opening crawl text and story proposals; typewritten letter from Veitch to Kennedy regarding their working partnership and relationship with Dark Horse, on top of which Veitch has written "Not Sent Per T.J."; Xeroxed art and scripts with autograph annotations throughout; 2 pp. change request from Dark Horse Comics; and shipping slips.

did.unittitle: "SW: DE II Issue #4," script and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II," nos. 3 and 4. Includes 4 pp. autograph panel layouts; printed typescript drafts of proposals for plot and opening crawls, with significant handwritten annotations and corrections; and Xeroxed artwork with and without lettering, with light handwritten annotations.

did.unittitle: "SEQUEL + DE final synopsis," Empire's End scripts and art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xerox drafts of work related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire: Empire's End." Autograph materials include 25 pp. saddle-stitched panel outline for "Empire's End," no. 1 and 25 pp. corner-stapled panel outline for "Dark Empire," no. 8. There are also 8 pp. panel layouts for "SWDE III Issue #1," presumably "Empire's End," no. 1; 5 pp. outline for "DE II Sequel"; and printed typescript and Xeroxed drafts of outlines for proposed "Empire's End" issues, including plot proposals, character proposals, and light correspondence related to these documents with significant autograph and holograph annotations throughout. Includes also the proposal draft for "Boba Fett Vs. The Empire," an unpublished story by Veitch that was rejected by Dark Horse Comics. With Xeroxed art and some color printed art.

did.unittitle: "SW: DE II Issue #5," script and illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire II," no. 5. Includes 25 pp. side-stapled autograph panel mockups, and 2 pp. autograph chapter notes. Printed typescript material includes scripts, proposals, and opening crawl text. Xeroxed panel art without script included.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 23 pp. Xeroxed artwork with lettering added for "Star Wars: Dark Empire" volume, "Empire's End," no. 2. Holograph annotations on Xerox.

did.unittitle: "SWDE," Empire's End #1-2 scripts and lettering dummy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph notes and printed typescript drafts of work related to "Star Wars: Dark Empire" volume "Empire's End." Significant autograph annotations to typescripts throughout, along with Xeroxed artwork on versos. Veitch has added sticky notes with additional information to many pages.

did.unittitle: "SWDE," Empire's End #2 scripts and lettering dummy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed and Xeroxed artwork, primarily from "Star Wars: Dark Empire" volume "Empire's End." Numerous autograph and holograph annotations and edits throughout. Includes printed email to Veitch from "Mike" commenting on "Star Wars" drafts in progress.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript drafts of public lectures given by Veitch regarding his writing for "Star Wars." Light autograph annotations throughout.

title.part: IMAGINING STAR WARS: How We Expanded George Lucas' Universe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript transcript of conversations about "idea for 'Star Wars' that [Veitch] came up with in 1988–1989 but never used." Autograph annotations throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript interview with Veitch regarding "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," likely intended for inclusion in Veitch's manuscript, "Imagining Star Wars: How We Expanded George Lucas' Universe." Autograph annotations throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 40 pp. printed typescript with autograph annotations. Excerpt from Veitch's manuscript, "Imagining Star Wars: How We Expanded George Lucas' Universe," primarily related to "Star Wars, Episode VII: The Force Awakens."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 pp. printed typescript excerpt from Veitch's long in-progress manuscript "Imagining Star Wars: How We Expanded George Lucas' Universe," a book-length project exploring Veitch's time writing "Star Wars" stories, analyzing the stories that came after, and discussing motifs throughout the films. Excerpt contains transcription of conversation between Veitch and several writers identified only by first name about plot lines and character development for "Star Wars, Episode VII: The Force Awakens."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 pp. printed typescript, corner-stapled. Likely an excerpt from "Imagining Star Wars: How We Expanded George Lucas' Universe." Contains plot summaries and notes from "Star Wars" movie "Rogue One" with autograph annotations throughout.

did.unittitle: Reviews of Disney-era Star Wars films

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pp. Xeroxed review from "Bennington Banner" reviewing Veitch's "The Visions of Elias," including original cutouts from newspaper. Includes also approx. 24 pp. printed typescript of reviews of "Star Wars" movies, including "Star Wars, Episode VIII: The Last Jedi" and "Solo: A Star Wars Story." There is also a 10 pp. printed typescript plot summary and analysis of "Solo: A Star Wars Story" with annotations by Veitch in red pen throughout.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and some Xeroxed materials related to Veitch's various "Star Wars" publications. Included are printed typescript drafts of Veitch's answers to interview questions, often with autograph annotations to the text; faxed written answers from Veitch and Kennedy to interview questions; biographical statement from Veitch; printed typescript interview "Questions for Kevin Anderson," possibly by Veitch, with light autograph annotation; and 2 printed typescript essay drafts by Veitch with autograph annotation throughout, titled "The Dark Secrets of Oz" and "Graffiti Seen on a Cantina Wall: Anakin Skywalker is a Mythological Wuss." Selection of Xeroxed material from magazine reviews of Veitch's work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph draft, 40 pp., of unpublished "Star Wars" novel "Lightsider" with handwritten revisions throughout. Includes also Xeroxed autograph draft, 31 pp., from an earlier version of the same autograph manuscript included in the folder, with minor differences from the autograph original.

did.unittitle: Complete draft, typed and numbered manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 120 pp. printed typescript draft of manuscript for Veitch's unpublished "Star Wars" novel, "Lightsider." Draft dated May 15 1993. Light autograph annotation on sticky notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black plastic three-ring binder, assembled by Veitch, containing approx. 120 pp. printed typescript draft of Veitch's unpublished "Star Wars" novel "Lightsider," draft dated May 15 1993. Additional 17 pp. printed typescript draft of first chapter is included in the back of the binder. Approx. 6 pp. autograph notes included in binder's front pocket, along with 3 pp. Xeroxed comments on "Lightsider" story by "Slap" and printed typescript response, likely by Veitch, corner-stapled together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 pp. printed draft of typewritten letter from Veitch to Mark Anderson, of Dark Horse Comics, with Veitch's annotations throughout; 5 pp. autograph draft of letter to Anderson from Veitch on legal-size paper; 2 pp. autograph notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 75 pp. printed typescript, Xeroxed magazine pages, and 2 pp. newsprint assembled by Veitch as reference material. Includes printed typescript glossary of "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," assembled by Bob Cooper, of Dark Horse Comics, along with printed typescript response by Veitch to the glossary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 pp. autograph notes and drafts of correspondence, as well as printed typewritten letter, signed, from Veitch to Mike Richardson of Dark Horse Comics, all related to proposed cancellation of Veitch's work with Dark Horse. Veitch notes that his "Boba Fett proposal...submitted in October was rejected" and that "DH would not welcome any further proposals."

did.unittitle: Tales of the Jedi / Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith / Tales of the Jedi: The Sith Wars

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for these projects in the notebooks within Series IV.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 2 linear inches of printed typescript, original pencil illustrations, Xeroxed artwork, and faxed artwork related to "Tales of the Jedi." Bulk of material includes Xeroxed drafts of artwork and panels by penciler Christ Gossett, many from issues no. 1, 2, 5, and 6. Several original pencil sketches by Gossett are included, as are printed typescripts by Veitch with proposals and outlines for respective issues and an autograph panel layout. Frequent autograph and holograph annotations and revisions occur throughout, including on Xeroxed material. There is also some printed typescript correspondence, along with several FedEx shipping slips documenting materials sent by Veitch to Gossett.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 2 linear inches of printed typescript, original pencil illustrations, Xeroxed artwork, and faxed artwork related to "Tales of the Jedi." Bulk of material includes Xeroxed drafts of artwork and panels by penciler Christ Gossett, many from issues no. 1, 2, 5, and 6. Several original pencil sketches by Gossett are included, as are printed typescripts by Veitch with proposals and outlines for respective issues and an autograph panel layout. Frequent autograph and holograph annotations and revisions occur throughout, including on Xeroxed material. There is also some printed typescript correspondence, along with several FedEx shipping slips documenting materials sent by Veitch to Gossett.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH," incomplete and early drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 65 pp. printed typescript, Xeroxed printed typescript, and Xeroxed artwork, most of which are drafts of proposals for the plot and characters of "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," nos. 1–12, co-authored by Veitch and Kevin J. Anderson. Autograph and holograph revisions occur throughout, along with some Xeroxed artwork. There is also outgoing correspondence from Veitch and from Anderson to Lucasfilm, with some shipping slips.

did.unittitle: Plot summaries for all issues and Skywalker Ranch photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 pp. printed typescript of timeline for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi"; 2 pp. Xeroxed fax response from Lucasfilm with answers for questions posed by Veitch while writing, likely "Tales of the Jedi"; 5 photographs from Veitch and Martha Veitch's visit to Skywalker Ranch, taken by Veitch and Martha; photograph of Veitch; 3 photographs of unidentified scenes; and Xerox cover from "Scenes from the Nativity" comic, featuring Rick Veitch as penciller.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 27 pp. Xeroxed mock-up of "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," no. 1, with autograph annotation throughout and sticky notes with additional autograph notes attached; 25 pp. Xeroxed mock-up of "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," no. 2, with light holographic annotations visible in the Xerox; Xeroxed autograph letter, signed, from Veitch to Dan Thorsland, of Dark Horse Comics; and printed typewritten letter, 2 pp., from Veitch to Chris Gossett.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," no. 4. Printed typescripts include panel and page breakdowns, scripts, and project proposals, with extensive autograph and holograph annotations throughout. Includes also Xeroxed artwork accompanied by script with handwritten revisions. Several FedEx shipping slips included, marking dates of exchanged material.

did.unittitle: Multi-issue illustrations and Dark Lords of the Sith #1 script

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Electric typescript and Xeroxed drafts of work for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi." Xeroxed drafts of artworks from issues no. 2, 4, and one unidentified issue, all without script, along with electric typescript draft of script for no. 1. Artists include Chris Gossett and David Roach. Housed in manila envelope, with corner-stapled Xerox of art and script for "Green Arrow."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 corner-stapled documents containing drafts of proposals, opening crawl text, and proposed plots for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," many with autograph and holograph revisions. Proposals sent largely by Veitch to Bob Cooper and Lucy Wilson, as well as to co-author Kevin J. Anderson. Includes Xeroxed, faxed typewritten letter, signed, from Anderson to Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proof copies and Xeroxed artwork with script for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," nos. 2–5. Frequent autograph and holograph annotations and revisions throughout by Veitch, Chris Gossett, and likely Kevin J. Anderson, Veitch's co-writer for the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proof copies and Xeroxed artwork with script for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," nos. 2–5. Frequent autograph and holograph annotations and revisions throughout by Veitch, Chris Gossett, and likely Kevin J. Anderson, Veitch's co-writer for the series.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH #2," scripts and illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 2. Includes: Xeroxed, faxes autograph letter, signed, from Veitch to Chris Gossett; Xeroxed, faxed typewritten letter, signed, from Veitch to Kevin J. Anderson and Gossett; typewritten letter, signed, from Anderson to Veitch with annotations by Veitch throughout; 2 pp. Xeroxed drafts of pages with art and dialogue; FedEx shipping slip from Veitch to Anderson; Xeroxed, faxed autograph letter, signed, from Veitch to Anderson with printed typescript attached; 6 pp. autograph notes on legal-size paper; 28 pp. faxed art mockup; 19 pp. Xeroxed art mock-ups with dialogue; and 24 pp. printed typescript draft of script. Significant autograph or holograph annotation throughout.

did.unittitle: "Bound Mock-up," Dark Lords of the Sith #1 (?) partial book dummy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 27 pp. printed mock-up of "Star Wars" comic without title, with black and white interior and color cover, bound in plastic wrappers. Veitch has placed sticky notes with annotations in red pen throughout.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH #1," scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and Xeroxed material, with some autograph material, related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 1. Includes 3 printed typescript drafts of script; Xeroxed printed typescript draft; printed typescript drafts of opening crawl text; 6 Xeroxed drafts of page layouts with complete art and scripts, one of which is side-stapled; Xeroxed drafts of illustrations, likely by Chris Gossett; and printed typescript and Xeroxed correspondence between Veitch and Kevin J. Anderson, related to edits and versioning between drafts. Autograph and holograph annotation is present throughout on majority of materials.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH #3," scripts and illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 3. Includes 4 printed typescript script drafts; 2 pp. autograph panel layouts, and some printed typescript and Xeroxed printed typescript correspondence between Veitch, Chris Gossett, and Kevin J. Anderson. There are also Xeroxed drafts of illustrations by Gossett, along with Xeroxed drafts of panels with illustrations and script. Holograph and autograph annotations present throughout.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH #4," scripts and illustrations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph, printed typescript, and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 4. Includes 7 pp. of autograph notes and 2 pp. autograph panel layout; 5 different printed typescript drafts of scripts for text, all with autograph annotations, along with printed typescript correspondence from Veitch to Kevin J. Anderson and Chris Gossett outlining changes and revisions to story and illustrations; 14 pp. printed typescript manuscript by Kevin J. Anderson titled "The Illustrated Star Wars Universe, Yavin 4"; 3 Xeroxed drafts of artwork with script, one of which is a partial version, with minor holograph annotations; and some shipping labels.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH #5," scripts and book dummies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 5. Includes printed typescript drafts of scripts; typewritten letter, signed, from Kevin J. Anderson to Veitch; different versions (including one side-stapled booklet) of Xeroxed mockup drafts with artwork and scripts; and shipping slips. Autograph and holograph annotations throughout on majority of materials.

did.unittitle: "TOJ 6 DLOTS #0," drafts of issues #6-7 The Freedon Nadd Uprising

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript and Xeroxed materials related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Freedon Nadd," no. 6. Includes 4 pp. autograph panel layouts; printed typescript drafts of scripts, plot proposals, and opening crawl text; and 4 25 pp. side-stapled panel layout mockups with script. Xeroxed drafts of panel artwork include autograph annotations throughout.

did.unittitle: "TOJ - Freedon Nadd," The Freedon Nadd Uprising #2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed typescript drafts and Xeroxed drafts of artwork. Includes mock-ups from "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising," no. 2, along with printed typescript drafts for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: The Sith War," no. 1. Autograph annotation to documents throughout along with some holograph responses. There is also a typewritten letter, signed, from Kij Johnson, of Dark Horse Comics, regarding Veitch's unpublished novel "Lightsider."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph notes, printed typescript, Xeroxed materials from "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," no. 5. Bulk includes Xeroxed artwork with scripts, often with autograph annotations. There are also 3 side-stapled 25 pp. panel mock-ups; a small selection of autograph notes; printed typescript drafts of scripts, opening crawl text, and proposals; and printed typescript and Xeroxed correspondence related to corrections and changes to draft materials.

did.unittitle: "[Dark Lords of the] SITH #6," scripts and storyboard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph notes and printed typescript with significant autograph annotation throughout, primarily including proposals for plot and development of "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 6. Edits to documents were shared between Veitch, Kevin J. Anderson, and Chris Gossett. Includes side-stapled autograph mock-up storyboard. Veitch has, at times, added sticky notes with autograph notes, including to the inside front cover of the folder.

did.unittitle: "DLOTS - Second Series," scripts and plot summaries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of proposals and plot outlines from Veitch and Kevin J. Anderson for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," primarily for nos. 7 and 8, as well as draft materials from "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: The Sith Wars." Typescript correspondence, primarily from Veitch and intended for fax to Anderson, focuses on critiques of work and drafts in progress. Includes 25 pp. saddle-stitched panel mockup layout; 12 pp. autograph responses to plot proposals written on legal-size paper; and shipping slips.

did.unittitle: "NEW STAR WARS / LUCAS 1998-1999 REF," early drafts for The Sith Wars

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Anderson attached; 10 pp. printed typescript draft of page breakdown for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi," no. 3, with light autograph annotation throughout; and 8 pp. autograph notes for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 2. Includes also Xerox mock-up with illustrations and script for "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith," no. 3.

Anderson following Veitch's earlier series "Dark Lords of the Sith." Veitch co-wrote scripts for the earlier issues in this series but was never officially credited.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 manila envelopes with carbon typescripts of "Dreams" manuscripts, in which Veitch recounts dreams by date. There are two different manuscripts, suggesting this was an experimental or ongoing exchange with Larry Goodell and Duncan McNaughton in particular. Envelope from Duende Press postmarked in 1974 contains several smaller corner-stapled drafts, and one postmarked from [Duncan] McNaughton in 1973 contains a large sheaf of dreams, one per page, from 1969 to 1973.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Files, including autograph notes, manuscripts, and other fragments for the development of a "Spiritual Dreaming Outpost" website. Includes printed typescript by "Martha Oz," as well as several quotations on writing that were likely displayed near a desk or workstation.

title.part: The Psyche As It Is: 400 Dreams of Transformation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early typescript fragment of "The Psyche As It is: 400 Dreams of Transformation," including dreams culled from the 1970s onward. With several printed typescripts from 2008 that attest to the beginning of this project, which pulled from Veitch's dream journals and selected dreams from specific time periods, expanding the project over time. Included also are typesetting notes on typeface and production.

Contains also autograph notes for a presentation on a similar topic, and a 5 pp. typescript with autograph notes dated 1962 containing the first few pages of the "Waking Up From God's Dreams" manuscript.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III is organized into four subseries: Dream journals, 1960s-1990s; dream journals, 2010s; spiritual awareness journals, and Martha Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series III contains 118 journals spanning 1962-2020. These journals primarily document Veitch's practice of dream analysis as well as his spiritual attitudes. At times, this content is blended with poetry, photographs, and ephemera.

Dream journals, 1960s-1990s, contain a sequence of personal journals maintained by Veitch from his time residing in New York City onward. Earlier content reads akin to a textual and visual diary of Veitch's life. In 1969, Veitch began using these journals to record his dreams. This practice, by 1973, would develop into decades of Jungian dream analysis. Veitch systematically dated each entry, in addition to paginating and numerically cataloging each journal, presumably to facilitate his own future reference to specific content. Entries consist of dream summaries accompanied by narrative analyses, which are marked by a dual perspective of Christian spirituality and psychological introspection. The consistency of this journaling practice slowed as Veitch's career in writing mainstream comics evolved. New entries temporarily ceased after 1993.

Dream journals, 2010s, represent the revival of Veitch's earlier practice in Jungian dream analysis. Veitch resumes dating the entries and restarts the pagination, though without the numerical cataloging scheme.

Spiritual awareness journals predominantly range from 1993-2011, placing this activity roughly between Veitch's earlier and later periods of dream journaling. This selection of journals restarts the pagination and is inconsistently cataloged. While the journals sometimes recount dreams, their content generally embodies a shift away from dream summaries and structured narrative analysis. In their place is a loose, poetic form containing lengthy discourses rooted in esoteric and spiritual traditions like alchemy, astrology, Eastern philosophies, and Catholicism. At times, Veitch describes these journal entries as "spiritual awareness poems" or "sutras."

Martha Veitch, wife of Tom Veitch, created her own dream analysis journals. They are recorded on loose leaf paper and are sporadically dated, with the most consistent sequence being from 1993-1997. Like Veitch, Martha employs Jungian dream analysis methods along with dated entries and a perspective marked by personal spirituality. Additional works by others are available in Series VI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 1. Title page reads "Apodictic Fists," and cover has an image of an embryo. 90 pp. of collage, typewritten and autograph poems and notes, photographs, and letters pasted in. Includes photographs of Veitch, Ted Berrigan, Dick Gallup, and others from 1962, an early photograph of Anne Waldman and Lewis Warsh, photograph of Joe Brainard, Martha Veitch (inscribed to Veitch), Veitch in monastic robes in 1968, and others. Some clippings pasted in include an interview with W.S. Burroughs, and early promotions of Berrigan's "The Sonnets." Includes a pasted-in typed poem near beginning of the book as part of a series: "Me. I'm dead, call me 'literature.' / They found all my notebooks in the drawers / but me, I'm dead, call me 'exponent of joy.'" Includes a bulletin from Weston Priory signed by the monks, including Elias and Veitch, for Easter 1968.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 2. 102 pp. of collage, autograph poems, drawings, correspondence, photographs, and other media. Includes photographs of Veitch and Martha Veitch, Lewis Warsh, and others. With poetry reading flyers, typescript poem by Dick Gallup in 1970, lists of brainstorming titles with Andrei Codrescu, and dream reflections including Bill Berkson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 3. The first of subsequent plain gray, sequentially-numbered notebooks through no. 20. Paginated 1–120. With collages, drawings, descriptions of dreams, photographs, and reading notes. One entry notes: "My own goal as a writer was to destroy literature. Now there will be no more literature. No more comics even. These words are the final dregs of philosophy...Goodbye all you word-guys! Poor bastards!" Includes work on "Deviant Slice" with Greg Irons, at "Rolling Stone," and drafts for "Big Jesus Comics #1."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, [no. 4]. Image of Hopper painting on cover of regular marbled notebook (the final such one in notebooks nos. 3–20). Paginated 121–358; pagination appears in red pen and was likely added at a date post-composition. Includes collage, autograph poems, reading notes, dreams, correspondence, and photographs. With typewritten letter, signed, from Ron Padgett mentioning "Antlers in the Treetops," photograph of Lewis Warsh, and photograph of Veitch and Greg Irons. Pp. 282–308 feature a prose story that has been stapled together. Includes ephemera related to underground comix convention in San Francisco, Last Gasp royalty statements, and other comics-related projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, with "May 1, 1973–May 23, 1974" on cover and spine. 29 pp. inventory of all dreams had in that year, with one to two-line summaries and annotation of patterns. Includes also a 3 pp. letter draft to Rick Veitch. Dreams are recorded in the notebooks numbered 1–20, and notebooks are cited in the catalog. Poets present in the dreams include Tom Clark, Bob Dylan, Florence Gude, John McAuliffe, Richard Waring, Ron Turner, Nancy Presson, Larry Fagin, Phoebe MacAdams, Lewis MacAdams, Ron Padgett, Bonnie Bromlett, Rebecca Wright ("Fury at Rebecca Wright who stole my yogurt and blueberries"), Brother Elias, Sonny Rollins, Jim Carroll, Greg Irons, Andrei Codrescu, and many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 5. Paginated 359–480. Illustrated cover, with a sword through a heart. Primarily explores Jungian psychology's intersection with alchemy and dreams, including Veitch's own notation of dreams along with collaged images. Includes reading list at back.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 6. Paginated 481–600. Illustrated cover, with alchemical bird. Focuses on alchemical process, with collaged alchemical images. Includes dream notation, typescript poem by Steve Winwood, clipping of Veitch quotation from "Rolling Stone," and drawings by Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 7. Paginated 601–724. Illustrated cover with image drawing from occult themes, including eye, cross, and moon motif in double triangle. Notes on dreams, collective unconscious, Goethe, and Joseph Campbell. Includes cover image of "The History of the Growth of Heaven" by Andrei Codrescu, whose original photograph is collaged in an earlier journal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 8. Paginated 725–846. Illustrated cover with tree deity, whose roots reach into water and with lotus hands and head. Mostly dream recordings and notes on dream-related readings, with some clippings and drawings. Back cover reads "If I had not found this man Jung, / what would I have done?" opposing a colorful mandala.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 9. Paginated 847–968. First page contains Veitch's birth chart, and note that he began Jungian analysis on November 4 1973. Primarily recordings of dreams, with some collaged elements from poetry readings and other sources, as well as several mandalas drawn and colored by Veitch. Veitch has revisited this book and noted mandalas of particular interest on the inside front cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 10. Paginated 969–1092. Primarily dream recordings with some collages; many of the dreams involved fellow poets. Includes photographic negative for offset printing laid in, with art likely by Greg Irons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 11. Paginated 1093–1216. Blue image of siren pasted to cover. Dream recordings, some collaged alchemical images, and colorful mandalas.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 12. Paginated 1217–1340. Dreams, mandalas, and diagrams of dream sites. Includes many references to poets and friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 13. Paginated 1341–1464. Dreams and mandalas, with notes on color theory. Many dreams are revisited in a different ink color with further notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 14. Paginated 1465–1586. Dreams, mandalas, and some collage, with typescript dream notes pasted in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 15. Paginated 1587–1710. Dreams and some mandalas, along with images of Tibetan deities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 16. Paginated 1711–1834. Dreams and small drawings, with some prayers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 17. Paginated 1835–1954. Dreams accompanied by small drawings and reflections, as well as reading notes on Jung and alchemy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 18. Paginated 1955–2077. Notes on Don Juan, fecal matter (poem), dreams, and extensive reflections on dreams as well as prayers and spiritual writing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 19. Paginated 2079–2200. Dreams, drawings, and collaged material, including typescript poem by Larry Fagin and Richard Manuel. Includes reading notes on the "Shadow" and other Jungian concepts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 20. Paginated 2210–2324. Dreams and notes, with some colorful illustrations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black notebook, no. 21. Paginated 2325–2607. Dreams recorded in typescript, and mostly two-column page system, as well as colorful mandalas (mostly near front of book), spiritual and religious writing, and other notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black notebook, no. 22. Paginated 2609–2892. Contains notes on Bubba Free John and Meher Baba, spiritual writings, dream recordings (extensively revisited in different ink) and drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black notebook, no. 23. Paginated 2893–3176. Dream recordings, reflections, writings, and drawings. On back page is a timeline of Veitch's life, year by year, from June 1959 to the final day of the journal, September 1 1975.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black notebook, no. 24. Paginated 3177–3460. With two of Veitch's drivers' licenses pasted on the inside front cover. Dream recordings, poems, notes, and extensive I Ching casting, including on whether Franklin Jones / Bubba Free John is the devil. List of teachers and gurus on p. 3294. On p. 3370, Veitch notes: "at this point I stopped recording my dreams" and offers an explanation of the rest of the contents of the notebook. Includes photographs of Veitch, and of Ron Padgett's house in Calais. Includes also notes about "The Luis Armed Story," publications and other poets, and projects. There are also I Ching castings for joining Bubba Free John's community.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black half-bound notebook with "Little League Baseball" sticker on front cover; no. 25. Paginated 3461–3616. Though Veitch indicated that he no longer recorded dreams regularly after November 1975, this notebook is paginated like the notebooks prior. Instead of dream reflections, it contains photographs of Veitch and Martha Veitch, clippings (of Ed Sanders and Tom Veitch, newspaper stories, and other items of interest), family photographs and photographs of friends' children, ideas for larger spiritual works, and aphorisms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 26. Does not continue pagination of no. 25. 92 pp. of collage, aphorisms, photographs, clippings, and other notes. Includes some poems by Angelica Veitch, and some ephemera and notes laid in but not yet collaged. Includes essay titled "I am the Ancient One. I Write Comic Books," among other writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, no. 27. With hot spring image on the back. Title page indicates that the work begins in the center and works out in both directions, and labels this book "#1," contrary to the 27 on the spine. 7 leaves in the front of the book, appearing to be from 1986 and involving dreams, are taped to the cover. 62 pp. of writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 1/2 x 5 1/2 in. grey notebook. Titled "February 23, 2015." 189 pp. of journaling, especially dream observations. Format of journals notes the time of sleeping and wakefulness, dreams in that time period, and a "notes" section with interpretation. There are also more general observations on waking life, including ongoing political and world events and current writing projects. Many times, observations end with invocation "So It Is," which appears in other journals. Continues in this fashion for 14 similar journals from 2015–2020.

The dream on p. 102–103 is noted as a "big dream," in which Veitch starts a new religion.

did.unittitle: "The Art of Dreaming" and "The Mind is Made Sacred"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 3/4 x 8 "National Diary for 1970" notebook. Table of contents indicates pp. 1–115 are "The Art of Dreaming," "written in Lake County, California in 1980–1981" and on p. 117, "The Mind is Made Sacred," identified as "spiritual awareness sutras, Volume One, August 1993 to May 1994" was written in Bennington, Vermont. 186 pp. total, with some autograph material copied and pasted. Some of the sutras were originally recorded in other notebooks, and these cases have been indicated by Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 x 5 1/4 in. black "record" book, with title page: "The Tree of Life." Several sections occur within this book, including poetry; teaching notes and class roster (with Simon Pettet, and other poets) for "Poetry and Spirituality"; personal reading lists; a list of Veitch's archive; notes on Kant, alchemy, and other spiritual practices; and notes for "Visions of a Poet." A dense notebook that draws many themes and practices together. Laid in are several photographs, including one of Veitch as a young child with the note: "the last time Tom ran away from home," with an image of him toddling down a sidewalk. Includes a postcard from Rick Veitch postmarked in 1975.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6 x 4 in. black notebook. Titled "The Long Awakening," "#4," "Winter 1993–1994." Inside cover notes that this item was re-copied January 1996 to "large journal #1." 181 pp., sutras.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 1/2 x 6 in. yellow spiral-bound notebook. Titled "Bedtime Notes (and R.E.M. scrawls)." Unlike most notebooks, unpaginated by Veitch. Contains poems and sutras. 73 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 1/2 x 4 in. "fat little wireless neatbook." On cover, notes "San Francisco April 13–28, 1994," and "#6." 35 pp. of sutras within; some pages were removed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 1/4 x 9 in. quarter-bound notebook, with print on cover signed by Angelica Veitch. Noted as "#7," May 1994, with 119 pp. of sutras.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. black notebook, with blue paper cover. Titled "Notes & Motes of the Rainbow Mind." Dated December 1994, San Francisco on the cover page, along with an "aum" sign and the note: "dedicated to the union of speech with the primordial nature." "#9." 176 pp. of poem drafts, and some dream notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. quarter-bound red book with paisley covers. Titled "Abhijananda's Journal" and dated August 14 1995 to November 11 1995 on title page, as well as "#12." Includes on cover page notes about "A Warrior's Dreaming" on p. 1, "Notes for a Fantasy Novel" on p. 11, and "The Opening of the World" on p. 13, as well as dates of composition. 158 pp. of poetry manuscripts, as well as note to insert "12a (gray record book) of Vermont trip, Aug–Sept 1995."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 1/4 x 5 1/2 in. cloth-bound notebook with title page: "In the Seat of Spiritual Power," dated April 1995. Notes this is notebook no. 10, and verso of title page notes dreams from 1995 with corresponding page numbers on which they are written. 155 pp. of autograph manuscript, mostly poetry with little editing from pp. 1–150 (as numbered by Veitch), and additional pages dated 2000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. quarter-bound blue book with paisley covers. Titled "Spiritual Science," and dated May 14 1995 on cover with note: "#11." 157 pp. poetry manuscript with titled works, and a few dream notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 x 7 1/2 in. grey "record" notebook. Titled "The Fairness Fulcrum," and noted "12a." From "Vermont Trip August 21–Sept 11 1995," with additional note: "These sutras go chronologically on page 30 of notebook #12." 46 pp. of autograph poems/sutras pasted onto rectos of pages, with additional notes on dreams interspersed. Referenced in August 14 1995 notebook (#12).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 1/2 x 4 in. "fat little wireless neatbook," blue. On cover, notes "Vermont Aug–Sept 1995" and "#13," as well as note: "used in #12." 33 pp. of assorted notes, including on HTML, patterns, cars, and other daily details.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. quarter-bound blue book with paisley covers. Titled "Mountain Sanctuary," dated November 12 1995 on cover page, and noted "#13" with sticky note. 160 pp. poetry manuscript, with many titled poems. "Mountain Sanctuary" is the last poem, suggesting that the book was named after its composition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. quarter-bound red book with paisley covers. Titled "Meditating Love's Apocalypse," and dated January 22 1996 on cover page. 158 pp. of spiritual poems, some with revisions in a different ink. Veitch has flagged certain poems for future use with sticky notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. black notebook. Titled "Light of Lights," and dated March 24 1996 on the cover page. 224 pp. poetry drafts, with some dated notes on dreams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. quarter-bound red book with paisley covers. Titled "God's Work" and dated "7/17/96" on title page. With epigraph: "until I awaken each day / in boundless joy, / my work is not done." 157 pp. of spiritual poems, including some autobiographical (such as p. 76, on Veitch's work in the 1980s).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. yellow utility sketchbook. Titled "The Mind of the Dolphin," and dated on title page September 16 1996–April 6 1997. 274 pp., sutras.

scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. dark green sketchbook. Titled "Truth is Our Power (Dissolving the Code of the Divider World)." Date range indicated on first page. 186 pp. of poems/sutras, some of which are associated with dreams, and some are marked with sticky notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. black sketchbook. Titled "The Perfection of Mind." Date range on first page. 182 pp. of poems/sutras, some of which are marked with sticky notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. dark green sketchbook. Titled "The New Earth." Date range on first page. 191 pp. poems/sutras, some of which are marked with sticky notes or annotated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. black sketchbook. Titled "A New Millennium of Grace." Date range on first page. 380 pp. of poems/sutras, ending at 4:30am on August 14 2000. Some marked with sticky notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 1/2 x 11 in. tan sketchbook, 382 pp., with front page titled "This Sacred House by the River." Autograph poems and dream notes, sometimes annotated "good one!" by Veitch or marked with sticky note. With the refrain: "So It Is" and "So Be It," and an overall thematic focus on spiritual teachings and epigraphs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 1/2 x 11 in. blue sketchbook, 382 pp. with front page titled "The Bardo of Waking." Contains autograph dream notes and poetry with ending refrain, "So It Is." Poems and dreams are titled and labeled, with sticky notes that mark particular passages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. orange sketchbook. Titled "Cessation and the Inseer," dated December 12 2022 to March 31 2004 on title page. 381 pp. autograph manuscript of spiritual poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. navy sketchbook. Titled "As It Is, So I Am" and dated April 2 2004–October 2 2004 on title page. 193 pp. of spiritual poems, many with cross icon as ending ornament.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2. navy sketchbook. Dated October 3 2004–March 25 2005 on title page, with epigraph from Tibetan Book of the Dead on title page. 193 pp. of poems/sutras, most of which end with "So It Is" or cross ornament.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 in. red book. Titled "A World in God." Date on title page: "April 7, 2005 to September 25, 2010." 431 pp. of autograph manuscript of poetry, with some dreams from 2009–2010 included. Poems show little signs of revision, though dreams show signs of revisitation with different inks. Contains notes in back for Old Bennington Books, Veitch's company. Includes also "Titles for Ron" on final page of book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 1/4 x 8 1/2 in. black sketchbook. Titled "September 26, 2010." 383 pp. of autograph notes with titled and dated poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 13 folders of dream journals, in autograph and printed typescript, by Martha Veitch. In addition to record of dreams, these documents share her reflections and memories of conversations with Veitch, especially on women and female energy, in the Jungian tradition, and her reflections on conversations with Veitch about dreams and shared Jungian philosophy.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series IV is organized into three subseries: Drafts, Star Wars , and administrative.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series IV contains a total of 74 notebooks spanning 1963-2019 that document professional and personal aspects of Veitch's life through literary drafts and administrative information.

Drafts encompass the largest number of notebooks in the series, totaling 58. Content relates to a wide range of Veitch's writings. Many notebooks in this sub-series contain drafts of comics, including underground works with Greg Irons and mainstream works commissioned by Marvel and DC Comics. Several "idea logs" provide insight into the earliest brainstorming stages of Veitch's comic drafting process. A significant portion of the notebooks relate to early works like The Planetary Route (unpublished, written 1969), in addition to esoteric and spiritual works like Visions of Elias: A True Story of Life in the Spirit (Manchester, Vermont: Sky River Books, 2016). The rest contain loosely organized drafts of prose and poetry.
Star Wars notebooks contain drafts relating to Star Wars comic projects.

Administrative notebooks document intersections between Veitch's career and personal life through notes on miscellaneous matters, including but not limited to business notes, an address book, and a personal reading list. See Series V for files retained by Veitch for administrative purposes.
For additional drafts relating to all genres and periods of Veitch's writings, see Series II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 1/2 x 11 in. paper box, labeled "Dreams Oct 63–Sept 73." 2 linear inches of typescript drafts of dream notes, with autograph notes at times. Illustrated cover page by Veitch and small illustrations within at times. Related to "The Book of Dreams."

did.unittitle: The Planetary Route and other notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 notebook, which is mostly complete, begins: "This book is a detailed record of the subjective spiritual life of one man. It is a report of dreams and visions, mundane and fantastic." After 28 pp., which reference numbered dreams to be inserted among Veitch's commentary, the first book switches to a detailed summary of "The Planetary Route."

relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

scopecontent.p: The notebook contains several pages of reflection on Veitch's time in the monastery, as well as drafts of letters regarding Adi Da (Bubba Free John). Over 100 pp. of writing.

scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 1/2 in. blue spiral-bound notebook with "Avatar Meher Baba" image and quotation pasted to cover. Contains draft of "God's Body" in typescript and autograph, draft of "The Planetary Route," and additional drafts of spiritual poems and spiritual works. Full notebook.

Jung," which has been crossed out to be retitled as "Visions of a Poet." 182 pp. of autograph, typescript, and cut-and-paste manuscript with significant revisions throughout. Includes APC from Bill Berkson laid in, dated January 7 1978.

scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, labeled "Crazy Monsters / New Toys" on spine. Contains scripts and plots in Veitch's hand, as well as drawings by Veitch and possibly Angelica Veitch. Includes also two rejection letters from Spielberg and Lucasfilm for unsolicited mail. Approx. half full.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, labeled "Idea Log #2" on spine. 114 pp. notes, which have mostly been collaged completely over by Veitch using headlines and news and magazine articles. The obscured manuscript appears to be a script or narrative regarding Jay, Grumm, and Setu—likely the unpublished "Grumm" comic developed with Steve Bissette. Pp. 88 onward are not obscured, and include notes on horror, cyborgs, and other superhero topics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Includes 160 pp. (completely full) autograph notes, typescript notes, clippings with annotation, revisions, and other plotting details related to the development of this comic with Cam Kennedy. Another composition notebook, labeled "The Light & Darkness War - Page Notes." Spine label indicates this is the second volume. 71 pp. of autograph notes and clippings regarding specific pages of the comic, as well as overall development research.

relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 3/4 x 5 3/4 in. yellow half-bound journal. Title page reads: "Notes for Novel(s) and Films—January 10 2005." 95 pp. of autograph character sketches, plots, and dreams. References continuation of ideas in another notebook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purple Staples wire-bound notebook, labeled "#1." 132 pp. of autograph notes, titled "Spiritual Life" on front page and continuing with additional poems and writings, some with titles. Some are attributed to Elias, and include dream visions.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red Staples wire-bound notebook, labeled "#2." 135 pp. autograph notes, including dreams, visions, and work attributed to Elias. Passages are revised, and contain references to the larger work, "Visions of Elias."

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dark green Staples wire-bound notebook, labeled "#3." 62 pp. of autograph notes, including dreams, visions, and passages attributed to Elias. With reference to "400 Dreams: Part 2.0," as well as sections titled by date and also theme.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

did.unittitle: Story concepts and notes on several projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 1/2 x 6 1/2 green notebook. 21 pp. of notes on projects, including notes transcribed from other notebooks. Subjects include "Star Wars," "The Light & Darkness War," and plots for various novels and movies, as well as notes on Veitch's visions.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for these projects in the writings within Series II.

did.unittitle: Books two, three, and five of Nikola Tesla project

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 yellow legal pads, entirely full. Contains script for a narrative involving Tesla as main character; many research files and notes on Tesla appear in the archive. With some revision by Veitch in a different pen color.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, labeled "Cranked or 'Crank It'" on Sky River Studios sticker. 8 pp. of notes on plot for fast-paced novel idea of Veitch's. In Angelica Veitch's former notebook; first and last pages are pasted down to remove her name from inside cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 1/2 x 8 in. purple spiral-bound notebook. One page: "Remembering 1962 / Ted Notes," with brief remembrance of a vibraphone. Notebook otherwise unpopulated.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 x 6 in. "speed ruled steno book." 69 pp. of autograph draft of prose work, with main characters Tom and Linda. Includes also "essay on authority," poem for Phoebe and Lewis (MacAdams), and other writings. Many pages have small psychedelic drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 x 6 in. "sterling top speed" steno book. 100 pp. of autograph draft of story about Tana Wind.

did.unittitle: Incomplete draft notebook for Star Wars comic projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yellow notebook with 13 pp. of autograph notes, drafts, and drafts of correspondence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for these projects in the writings within Series II.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, labeled "Log X" on spine. Full notebook (160 pp.) of ideas and notes on Jedi chronicles, "Star Wars," notes from meeting at comic shop on "Star Wars," notes from phone calls with collaborators, and new ideas on the "Star Wars" universe and series.

did.unittitle: Draft notebook for Star Wars comic projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black leather-bound notebook with autograph drafts of works, plot ideas, and story notes by Veitch related to "Star Wars," including both "Dark Empire," no. 3 and "Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith." 86 pp. of autograph writing with some printed typescript tipped in. Annotations in red pen by Veitch.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for these projects in the writings within Series II.

did.unittitle: Dark Lords of the Sith notebook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Medium green Staples wire-bound notebook, with 16 pp. of autograph notes, including notes on Jedi characters, Nomi Sunrider, and other Star Wars-related themes, including revision in red pen.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

did.unittitle: Draft notebook for Star Wars comic projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black leather-bound notebook containing approx. 171 pp. autograph material by Veitch with some newsprint and printed typescript tipped in, often with annotation by Veitch in red pen. With extensive autograph notes and studies on the original "Star Wars" cinematic trilogy by Veitch, this notebook was likely shared with Cam Kennedy in preparation for writing of "Dark Empire." It contains significant writing and brainstorming throughout on themes and motifs of the "Star Wars" series, with Veitch returning to his own writing later in red pen to further supplement his reflections. Annotations explore themes of Buddhism, Cormac McCarthy, and Jung, and other archetypes in Lucas' films.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red spiral-bound Fays Quality notebook with autograph title on cover. 32 leaves of autograph notes about plot, characters, and correspondence related to "Star Wars, Tales of the Jedi."

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See related drafts for this project in the writings within Series II.

did.unittitle: Incomplete notebook referencing The Last Jedi film

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Teal Staples wire-bound notebook with 2 pp. of autograph notes, including administrative notes and one page of script involving Leia from Star Wars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 pp. autograph reflections on Cam Kennedy collaboration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tan notebook, "Grape Press / Pants Press." Contains 13 pp. of autograph notes for sales of Grape Press books, including "Tom Veitch Magazine." The other side of the notebook is titled "Dreams August 1970," and contains 15 pp. of autograph dream notes. Several additional items of publishing ephemera and dream notes laid in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11 x 8 in. blue notebook, titled "Booklist." 16 pp. of reading lists, including one by W.S. Burroughs, Veitch at Naropa in Summer 1978, and books from Jung's bibliographies, as well as authors influenced by Jung. Other reading lists are also contained in journals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, "Names & Addresses." A jam-packed homemade address book with autograph names/addresses, business cards tipped in, some clippings, and other reference. For comics sales, collaborators, reviews, solicitations, and all manner of contacts. Some mail laid in addressed to Elias Oz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Composition notebook, "Business Notes and Taxes." 66 pp. of notes on contracts, discussions about taxes, author benefits, and other details, as well as production schedule for Veitch in the early 1990s as created by him. An interesting glimpse into the practical and financial considerations of his creative practice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 x 5 in. spiral-bound notebook picturing Northeast states on cover, with arrow towards Vermont and circle around New York City. Contains 35 pp. of autograph jottings, including notes on computers, house purchases, Veitch practicing his signature, some notes on Star Wars, and other intersections of life logistics and inspiration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Yellow legal pad, approx. half full, with task lists, project notes, and reflections on Veitch's 78th birthday. Includes also notes on brother Michael Veitch's case against a visiting priest at Weston Priory, pursuing justice for an assault inflicted on him during a 1970 visit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 x 5 in. tan notebook with comic pasted to front. Several pages of research within on synthesizer purchases, including latest models and features. Small note on DC Comics contract draft.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12 x 9 in. spiral-bound sketchbook, with 20 pp. pencil and pen and ink drawings, and photocopy of drawing signed by Tom Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series V contains a small extent of files documenting Veitch's administrative affairs and research into various topics. Generally, the files comprise a mix of ephemera, clippings, photographs, and notes.

Examples of featured administrative subject matter include mailing lists, event invitations, museum membership, local bookstores in Vermont, and logo designs for Veitch's Sky River Books imprint. There is also a Rolodex of cards identifying Veitch's personal and professional contacts; see the address book in Series IV for similar information.

Subjects of Veitch's research are wide-ranging in topic and often lack clear associations with particular projects. Reference materials relate to subjects like politics and current events, cults, historical images, and vernacular language. For additional files that Veitch used for research, see Series VI.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder of selective clippings of unusual news and stories that would likely have been collaged into inspiration journals that Veitch kept from the 1980s onward. Includes also postcard of Tatooine and small autograph note on "Star Wars."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several pages of mailing lists for national and local press, likely related to comics work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding "poet's dinner" at Columbia College, honoring Allen Ginsberg, Ron Padgett, Paul Auster, Kenneth Koch, and many other Columbia College alumni from the early 1960s. Includes Veitch's letters to the Dean, as well as Ron Padgett and Allen Ginsberg, expressing his distaste for the organization of the event and his refusal to participate. Includes APC from Ron Padgett as a follow-up on September 18 1990. With interesting ephemera, including guest lists.

did.unittitle: Notes on Lightmind Forum, Star Wars , and other projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter of solicitation from the American Museum of Natural History, inviting associate membership status.

did.unittitle: Inuit sled dogs article from the Wall Street Journal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assembled by Veitch. Folder of local ephemera from nearby bookstores, collected during Veitch's time as a bookseller. A portrait of independent bookselling in Vermont.

did.unittitle: Bellows Falls High School class of 1959 reunion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder for Veitch's 50th reunion, Bellows Falls High School. Includes correspondence with a Vietnam War veteran named Stan, and several copies of material noted "for Lew McA," presumably referencing Lewis MacAdams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 pp. printed typescript titled "California Suite," with handwritten revisions. Poetry and/or song lyrics by Tom Veitch.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VI is organized into two subseries: Screenplays and other works, and Star Wars .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VI contains works created by people other than Veitch. The materials span circa 1971-2016. It is likely that many of these files were retained by Veitch for their value as reference material for his writings.

Screenplays and other works encompass several forms of materials. Most files feature screenplays or scripts for ten films dating from the 1980s-1990s. Other files contain collected manuscripts by others, including literary drafts from friends and acquaintances during the 1970s, several reviews and essays, and a 2008 proposal for an anthology of academic monographs. There is also an annotated copy of The Da Upanishad by Adi Da Samraj (Clearlake, California: Dawn Horse Press, 1989). Additional materials related to Samraj and the Adidam religious movement are available in Series IX.

The Star Wars subseries contains materials related to Star Wars movies, television series, novels, and comics. This includes multiple draft versions of screenplays and scripts for Star Wars movies. Nearly all of the documentary content would have been available to Veitch while he was writing official Star Wars comics.
For additional works by others, see Martha Veitch's journals in Series III.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "OINK" folder with label by Veitch. Contains manila envelopes with manuscripts for the following: "Thunder Road" by Hitlon [sic] Obenzinger (typescript draft); "Running Gun" by David Amdison with note to Veitch: "Tom—sorry I couldn't do anything to improve it." (typescript draft with handwritten revisions); Bill Berkson's "Phenomenological Wheels" (inscribed to Veitch with original collage elements); Lewis Warsh's "Chestnut Mare" (with original collage and autograph letter to Warsh, inscribed to Veitch and Martha); Larry Fagin's "Brain Damage" (mimeographed typescript); and George Mattingly and Tim Hildebrand's "The Rick Nixon Story" (accompanied by typewritten letter, signed, wondering about Greg Irons as an illustrator).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed at Script City, Hollywood, bound with duct tape. Autograph note on front cover: "Body Heat playing time: 113 mins 205 scenes." Contains Xeroxed third draft, dated October 6 1980, of original screenplay by Lawrence Kasdan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed at Script City, Hollywood. One copy is a Xerox of John W. Rogers' copy of the storyboards for "Blade Runner"; Rogers was the production manager of the film that was released in 1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed at Script City, Hollywood, bound with brass fasteners and duct tape. Autograph note on front cover: "Wall Street playing time: 124 mins 192 scenes." Contains Xerox of original screenplay by Stanley Weiser & Oliver Store. Third draft with several revisions.

did.unittitle: David Huxley, "Naked Aggression: American Comic Books and the Vietnam War," in Tell Me Lies About Vietnam: Cultural Battles for the Meaning of the War , eds. Alf Louvre and Jeffrey Walsh (Philadelphia: Open University Press)

Screenplay by David Lynch, based on the novel by Barry Gifford. Xeroxed screenplay copied from no. 177, revised first draft August 2 1989.

scopecontent.p: Unbound script with 3-hole punched papers. Front cover reads: "Judge Dredd by William Wisher staring [sic] S. Stallone as Dredd." Xeroxed version of script, with no version, date, or printer information. Veitch had housed in plastic bag.

Contains Xeroxed copy of "Alien III" by Walter Hill and David Giler, with 9 sets of revisions from January 16 1991 to April 10 1991. These revisions should be noted by color in the manuscript, which was copied in black ink.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: The Light and Darkness War review by Stephen R. Bissette for 2015 collected edition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unbound script with 3-hole punched papers. Front cover reads "Burning Chrome by William Gibson." Xeroxed version of script, with no version, date, or printer information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black plastic three-ring binder, assembled by Veitch, with approx. 1 linear inch of Xeroxed typescript and comic book material. Includes 13 pp. 1973 draft of proposed story "The Star Wars," by George Lucas; 26 pp. 1983 draft of screen treatment for "Star Wars, Episode III: Fall of the Republic," by John L. Flynn, a fan work sent to Lucas which Lucas declined to produce but authorized for distribution; official glossaries of terms related to "Star Wars"; excerpt of comic book written by Archie Goodwin; collaged glossary of official "Star Wars" posters, created by Veitch, likely as reference material; and 30 pp. printed "Star Wars" comic. This assemblage was likely created in the early 1990s, though the contents of the binder were created prior.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Veitch has placed three sticky notes on pages in the script, one with autograph notes, and annotated the cover.

Provided to Veitch at the behest of George Lucas as research for "Star Wars: Dark Empire." Title page notes fifth draft, dated February 1979.
