Majority of material consists of story proposals by Timothy Zahn for "Star Wars: The Saga Continues, Book One."

did.unittitle: Zorba the Hutt's Revenge young adult novel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 80 pp. Xeroxed typescript draft of "Zorba the Hutt's Revenge," a young adult "Star Wars" novel by Paul Davids and Hollace Davids. Red holograph annotations by Barbara Kesel throughout. Sent to Veitch by Kesel.

did.unittitle: Timothy Zahn, Star Wars: The Last Command manuscript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximaxately 529 pp. Xeroxed printed typescript novel draft of "Star Wars: The Last Command" by Timothy Zahn. Padding provided in box with a newspaper dated 1993.

did.unittitle: Michael Kaminski, "Early Treatment of the Phantom Menace"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 17 pp. Xeroxed printed typescript summarizing "A Look at the Rough Draft of Episode I," later released as "Star Wars, Episode I: The Phantom Menace." Light autograph annotation throughout.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VII is arranged chronologically. Albums have been aggregated under their own heading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VII contains photographs taken or collected by Veitch throughout his life. There are approximately 800-1000 individual photographs featuring images captured predominantly from the 1970s-1990s.

All formats appear to be traditional film photography. Most are chromogenic prints between 3½" and 4"x6," although some prints are as small as 3½"x3½" and as large as 8"x10." A handful of miscellaneous sized black-and-white prints are present. Corresponding film negatives are available for many of the prints in either 35mm color or 45mm black-and-white formats. Nine disk film negatives are also present.

Regarding image content, the photographs are thorough documentation of Veitch's personal life. Veitch's youth in Bellows Falls, Vermont, is depicted in a 1940s-era family photo album. A wide variety of locations are represented, including Veitch's residences in the San Francisco area (circa 1970s) and Bennington, Vermont (circa 1980s onward), in addition to his travels in Scotland (1988), California and Skywalker Ranch (1990-1991), Florida (1991), and London (circa 1989-1992). Many photographs lack identifying captions on their versos and have been mixed with images from different film rolls, thereby obscuring information on further dates and locations.

Veitch's family and friends appear often in the images, especially his wife Martha (1969 onward) and their daughter Angelica. A booklet titled "The Penicuik Book" resulted from a 1988 trip to the Scottish town of Penicuik, the ancestral home of the Veitch family.
Other personal subject matter includes Veitch with cars – reflecting his acumen as an auto mechanic in the 1980s – and a file of "photo work" attesting to Veitch's technical studies in photography.

Some image content intersects with Veitch's professional life. Scottish comic artist and illustrator Cam Kennedy, a key collaborator with Veitch on The Light and Darkness War (1988) and Star Wars: Dark Empire (1991-1992), appears in photographs taken in Scotland and Bennington, Vermont. Another notable aspect concerns images likely captured as reference material for writing projects, including museum exhibitions of modern and historical arms and armor, Star Wars realia, and, on the disk negatives, reproductions of natural and religious collection material from the Smithsonian Institution.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Photographs are filed to reflect existing groupings as received. Within files, photographs are sequenced in the order as received regardless of whether the grouping contains images mixed from different film rolls.
While many photographs have been removed from their original enclosures, these enclosures have been retained for their inscriptions and as evidence of how Veitch arranged his photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black album, with "Photographs" gold-stamped on cover. Contains 18 photographs from Veitch's youth, circa 1940s. Many photographs from the album have been removed.

did.unittitle: Travels to Scotland, Skywalker Ranch, and with Cam Kennedy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Album with mostly black and white photographs of Veitch and Cam Kennedy. Some photographs laid in, from Skywalker Ranch visit in 1991, as well as more recent photograph (circa 2010s).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several dozen photographs that were originally unfoldered. Includes photographs of family, Angelica and Martha Veitch, Bubba Free John, and mostly Veitch. Includes photograph of Veitch and Greg Irons circa 1970s, as well as early photograph of Veitch circa late 1960s. Collection of photographs noted "special photographs" contains primarily family photographs, with some identification on the back. Several photographs of Veitch working with vintage cars over the years, and collection of photographs from early 1970s (up to 1973) of Veitch and Martha, with baby Angelica Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder assembled by Veitch. Contains several printed typescript biographical notes, promotional card from comic reading, contact sheet from 1970s, images of Veitch and collaborators in several dozen photographs, Veitch in "Batmon" shirt with cigar and other headshot-type images, and some negatives of promotional images.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 packets of photographs, some with negatives included. Includes friends and their houses, photographs of "Star Wars" Skywalker Ranch, downtown Bennington Vermont, trips to San Francisco, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 packets of photographs, some with negatives included. Includes friends and their houses, photographs of "Star Wars" Skywalker Ranch, downtown Bennington Vermont, trips to San Francisco, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disk negatives mostly include reference images of Smithsonian holdings, likely for drawing reference, as well as visit by Cam Kennedy to Bennington, VT. Photographs include friends, art references, Martha Veitch, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disk negatives mostly include reference images of Smithsonian holdings, likely for drawing reference, as well as visit by Cam Kennedy to Bennington, VT. Photographs include friends, art references, Martha Veitch, and family.

did.unittitle: Binder pages of negatives relating to family life and travel

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brown leather binder, with photographs from Penicuik, Scotland, where Veitch's father was raised. 61 photographs, labeled with landscapes and including family gatherings. Some were likely removed for "The Penicuik Book," a gift to Veitch's mother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 19 pp. small booklet with marble covers, containing photographs and typescript (both tipped in). Inscribed to "Mom Xmas 1988" from Veitch and Angelica Veitch. Contains photographs of Penicuik, Scotland, where Veitch's father was from. With photographs of old family headstones from 19th century family members, narrated by Veitch to his mother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red binder, "Photo Work." Contains reference studies from 1989 as Veitch experimented with capturing quality photographs (from a technical standpoint), including Angelica Veitch as test subject for several shots. In addition to this are 14 photographs of tattoos by Greg Irons, and 3 photographs of Tom Veitch.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs were printed in mostly July and August 1990, and include friends, art references, Martha Veitch, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs were printed in mostly July and August 1990, and include friends, art references, Martha Veitch, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs were printed in mostly July and August 1990, and include friends, art references, Martha Veitch, and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder assembled by Veitch, titled "Photographs." Several dozen photographs, including images of family, Veitch, nature, their house in VT, travels (to San Francisco), and several large prints of Veitch with Chewbacca at Skywalker Ranch, taken by Martha Veitch.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series VIII is arranged chronologically. Oversize materials have been aggregated under their own hierarchical heading.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series VIII contains artworks, prints, and ephemera across different phases of Veitch's literary career.

Most material relates to comic projects, including The Light and Darkness War (1988), Star Wars: Dark Empire (1990-1991), Clash (1991), Flenk's Last Tattoo (unpublished) with Greg Irons, Grumm (unpublished) with Stephen Bissette, and projects with artist Adam Kubert. Additional subjects include art for Tom Veitch Magazine (1970-1971), marketing ephemera for comics and other literature, and reference material for graphic and literary works by Veitch and others.
Oversize material features similar subject matter but with dimensions greater than 14"x18." The largest among these selections is a 22"x34" broadside poster announcing the publication of Clash .
See Series II for additional graphic materials related to the writings in this series.

did.unittitle: Tom Veitch Magazine transparencies and stencils

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 large photographic negatives used for offset printing of "Tom Veitch Magazine," 1 measuring 10 x 12.5 in., 2 measuring 9 x 12 in.

did.unittitle: Tom Veitch Magazine #4 cover art and unattributed watercolor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artworks created for "Tom Veitch Magazine" cover: drawing with blue and purple marker, 11 x 14 in., with 5.5 x 8.5 drawing in blue marker tipped on, glue detached; photographic negative reproduction of previous drawing, used for printing; watercolor, 12 x 11 in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manila envelope with "Rip Off Press" sticker to flap containing 2 photographs, both with autograph instructions for size and toning (one depicts women walking with baskets of laundry balanced on their heads, the other is of the poet Michael Brownstein); 14 paste-ups for "Tom Veitch Magazine," no. 4, 1971, the majority of which are approx. 12 x 17 in. with some variation (one measures 9 3/4 x 14 in.), which were used to produce offset prints. These paste-ups include a cover paste-up with description of magazine's contents; an excerpt of "Eat This!"; and layouts of the "Letters to the Editor" section, containing original typescript and autograph letters from Ron Padgett, Alan Bernheimer, John Allegro, Jeff Nuttall, Ken Mikolowski, and W.S. Burroughs, among many others.

did.unittitle: Reference file of visual works and news clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 27 photographs, prints, dust jackets, and newspaper clippings saved by Veitch as reference for work or preservation. Items range from 13 x 19 in. to 4.5 x 7 .5 in. Clippings feature mentions of "Tom Veitch Magazine," "The Mick Jagger Story," an essay by Veitch, and "Sight for Sore Eyes," a poem by Anne Waldman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder created by Veitch, with clippings (of published drawings by Greg Irons, religious figures, and other images) that would likely have been collaged into journals. Importantly, also contains alchemical designs that appear on the cover of his 1970s notebooks; these have been traced and drawn by Veitch from Xeroxes in sheets of symbols. With Veitch's original artwork.

did.unittitle: Lettered prints by Adam Kubert for unidentified comic project
Star Wars: Dark Empire advertising poster and Return of the Jedi art print

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8.5 x 11 in. offset print featuring Jabba the Hutt; 9.5 x 14 in. Xerox poster advertising the publication of "Star Wars: Dark Empire."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A 22 pp., 11 3/4 x 16.5 in. Xerox copy of "De Archivaris," with by pencils by François Schuiten and story by Benoît Peeters, absent the lettering of dialogue; 5 pp., 11 x 17 in. Xerox of "Thought-Man" comic, illustrated by Joe Brainard, inscribed to Veitch by Ron Padgett: "12/6/19 Tom - Recognize this? Ron," and housed in a white Teachers & Writers Collaborative envelope; 5 pp., 11 x 17 in., Xeroxed copy of "Sgt. Rock Spectacular," no. 7 by Robert Kanigher and Ric Estrada housed in manila envelope, posted to Veitch's address in San Francisco from Dover, NJ, and housed with "Time" magazine cover; and 11 x 17 in. Xerox poster advertising Veitch's appearance at Hampshire Mall in Hadley, Massachusetts, February 29 1992.

did.unittitle: The Light and Darkness War reference material and sketch map
The Light and Darkness War designs and marketing
Prints and page mockups for various comic projects

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10.5 x 16 in. newsprint reproduction of artwork by Greg Irons; 13 3/4 x 11 in. Xerox reproduction of artwork by Greg Irons; 11 x 17 in. Xerox reproduction of artwork featuring a man walking through falling human body parts and football helmet, no artist attributed; 2 8.5 x 11 in. color Xerox reproductions of characters' headshots, artist unattributed; 7 x 17 in. Xerox reproduction of 1989 artwork by penciler Jim Calafiore; 10 pp. 11 x 8.5 in. Xerox reproductions of comic book panels, many in landscape format with artists unidentified, 1 p. inscribed to Veitch from Adam Kubert, 1 p. identified as "Sunday Night Fever"; 11 pp. 8.5 x 11 in. Xerox reproductions of proofs for "Lobo: Bounty Hunting for Fun and Profit" by Adam Grant and Frank Gomez with holograph titling and numbering present on the original throughout; 10 pp., 11 x 17 in. Xerox reproductions of offset masters with art by Frank Gomez from various comics including "Superman: At Earth's End" and "Lobo: Bounty Hunting for Fun and Profit," among others; 17 x 11 in. Xeroxed reproduction of concept art for character "Rattank - armored terrorsaur," artist unattributed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several examples of original artwork, Xerox copies at different scale, and other production files related to development of the Grumm title. Grumm was an unpublished collaboration with acclaimed comic artist Steve Bissette.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8.5 x 11 in. offset print, featuring a cartoon portrait of Tom Veitch by Greg Irons. This is no. 39 from an edition of 75 copies, signed by Veitch. There is also a 14.5 x 11 in. diptych of "Slo.Death/Slow Death," comic covers by Greg Irons and Richard Corben (identified as "Gore"); 8.5 x 10 in. photographic print of "My Name Is Chaos" #3 cover art; 12 x 19 in. offset poster for Holy Ghost, band featuring Mike Veitch and Peter Veitch; 15 x 11 in. offset print, "Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles," by Steve Bissette, out of 100, unnumbered; and 2 offset prints, 11 x 17 in., by Frank Frazetta.

did.unittitle: Flenk's Last Tattoo illustrations by Greg Irons, removed from "Comics illustrations"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 pp., 14 x 20.5 in. Xeroxed drafts of illustrations from "Flenk's Last Tattoo," by Veitch and Greg Irons, with holograph revisions on the original present on several pages. These were the rough sketches of this unfinished final work that Irons and Veitch were pursuing at the time of Irons' death.

did.unittitle: The Light and Darkness War #1-2 comic art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 7 x 10.5 in. watercolors signed by Adam Kubert, accompanied by acetate overlay for printing, housed in manila envelope. These are striking artworks that show the interaction between hand-coloring and offset printing of comics. Includes also 6 pieces of ephemera from "The Light & Darkness War," with paste-up announcing issue no. 4 and original marker illustration, unsigned, announcing Veitch as new writer for "Star Wars" comic series; 4 11.5 x 16 in. pages of comic art without dialogue taped together, housed with "The Light & Darkness War" poster in folder with holograph illustrations to inside covers; approx. 34 color prints from "The Light & Darkness War," ranging from 12 x 20 in. to 11 x 17 in. with bulk 11 x 17 in., each signed by Cam Kennedy and Tom Veitch, each numbered from editions of varying sizes, housed with 3 Xerox prints from "The Light & Darkness War" and 1 8.5 x 11 in. color print from the series. Veitch has used sticky notes to mark which issue each print is from. This folder contains prints from nos. 1 and 2.

did.unittitle: The Light and Darkness War #3-4 comic art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approx. 42 prints from "The Light & Darkness War," numbered and signed by Veitch and Cam Kennedy. This portfolio, with autograph sticky notes, contains prints from issues 3 and 4. Most prints measuring 11 x 17 in., with some 11 x 20 in., and an unsigned print measuring 22 x 17 in., folded once.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 22 x 34 in. poster announcing publication of Veitch and Adam Kubert collaboration Clash .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 22 x 14 in. screenprint with blue ink on silver cardstock, with art by Greg Irons depicting Veitch as a monk, announcing 3 readings by Andrei Codrescu, Tom Veitch, and Philip Whalen, sponsored by Yellow Press.
